line_item_fields.name: Job Name (or "Lamex PO" on some receipts)

validation_rules: If totals exist: total ~= subtotal + tax
If line items exist: total ~= sum(line_items.amount)
instructions:
Extraction Template Instructions:

1. Vendor:
   - Anchor to store/company name at top or prominent location. Common patterns: uppercase vendor names, “Sold by”, “From”, or branding.
   - For Lowe's, match lines with “LOWE’S HOME CENTERS, LLC” or similar.

2. Invoice/Receipt Number:
   - Look for label variants: “Invoice No”, “Receipt #”, “Transaction #”, “Order No”, “Bill No”.
   - Extract the value directly to the right or below. If missing, set as blank.

3. Transaction/Invoice Date:
   - Anchor to date near the top, labeled or standalone. Formats may include MM/DD/YY, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD.
   - Normalize to YYYY-MM-DD. If year is two digits, prefix with “20”.

4. Invoice/Receipt Subtotal:
   - Locate labels: “Subtotal”, “Amount Before Tax”, “Merchandise Total”, or first summary amount.
   - Strip currency symbols ($, etc) and commas. Extract numeric value.

5. Tax:
   - Anchor to nearby “Tax”, “Sales Tax”, or “VAT”. Extract value as string (can be blank if absent).

6. Invoice/Receive Total:
   - Locate labels: “Total”, “Grand Total”, “Amount Due”, last summary amount.
   - Strip currency symbols and commas. Extract numeric value.

7. Service Location, Shipping Address, Billing Address:
   - Look for labeled blocks: “Service Location”, “Shipping Address”, “Bill To/Billing Address”. Extract block of text immediately following label.
   - Return blank if not present.

8. Handwritten Note(s):
   - If field is provided distinctly or labeled “Notes”/“Handwritten”, extract. Otherwise, leave blank.

9. Document Name:
   - Use PDF filename present at bottom/top or metadata (e.g., "CDII_004888.pdf"). If unavailable, leave blank.

10. Page Number:
    - Look for “Page X of Y”, “Page:”, or similar footer/header indicators.

11. Bates Number:
    - If present, extract strings in Bates stamp format or labeled “Bates”, often in footer/header.

Line Items Extraction:

- Table Detection: Identify sections with repeating product or service lines, usually a vertical list with item names and prices.
- Define header or pattern: First column (description), last column (amount/price). Ignore non-line items or promotional listings.
- Item Description: Capture full product/service text from the line.
- Item Amount: Extract closest price on each line; strip currency and commas.
- Job Name/Lamex PO: If a column or text labeled “Job Name”, “Lamex PO”, or similar exists per line, extract value; otherwise, leave blank.

Normalization Rules:
- Dates: Convert all to YYYY-MM-DD.
- Numbers/Currency: Remove commas and symbols ($, etc), return as float.
- String fields: Strip surrounding whitespace.

Always return blank ("") for required fields if target label or value is not present/unreadable.
output_columns: Job Name (or "Lamex PO" on some receipts)
